title: Melted Gooey Moroccan Fish Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Moroccan dish featuring fish cooked in a mixture of sesame seed oil and render fat, resulting in a bitter and gooey texture, perfect for a pescatarian meal prep option.
instructions: Heat the sesame seed oil in a fondue pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the cod fillets and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the render fat, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 10 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in any accumulated juices and serve hot.
Serve with crusty bread or over couscous.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
Let cool, then refrigerate or freeze for later meal prep.
